agent_output: {"executive_summary":"Multi-region logistics platform architecture expanding from single-region EU to EU + US + APAC. The design uses region-sovereign data planes for GDPR/CCPA compliance, a lightweight global control plane for cross-region coordination, and an asynchronous event mesh for the global operations dashboard. Each region is a self-contained deployment that owns its customer and courier data, enforces geo-fencing at the domain level, and can degrade gracefully during outages. The global layer is read-only and aggregation-focused — it never stores PII.","architecture":{"components":[{"name":"Regional Data Plane (x3: EU, US, APAC)","responsibility":"Full-stack deployment per region: API gateway, application services, database, cache, message broker. Owns all customer PII, courier assignments, and order lifecycle for that region. Serves < 100ms latency via regional proximity.","boundaries":"Hard boundary at region. No cross-region synchronous calls. Customer and courier data never replicated outside the region. Each plane is independently deployable and can operate in isolation."},{"name":"Regional API Gateway","responsibility":"Routes customer/courier requests to regional services. Enforces authentication, rate limiting, and geo-routing. Performs initial geo-validation — rejects requests that arrive at the wrong region.","boundaries":"Region-scoped. Sits behind a global DNS/CDN layer that routes by geography."},{"name":"Global DNS + CDN Layer","responsibility":"GeoDNS (e.g., Route 53 latency-based routing) directs customers to their nearest regional API gateway. CDN caches static assets globally.","boundaries":"No PII passes through CDN. DNS is the only truly global routing component."},{"name":"Global Control Plane","responsibility":"Lightweight service for cross-region coordination: region health monitoring, global configuration distribution, region-to-region escalation (e.g., support tickets that cross regions). Stores NO customer PII — only anonymized operational metadata.","boundaries":"Read-only access to regional event streams. Cannot write to regional data stores. Deployed multi-region for its own availability."},{"name":"Global Operations Dashboard (Aggregator)","responsibility":"Consumes anonymized/aggregated metric streams from all regions. Provides real-time KPIs: deliveries in progress, SLA compliance, courier utilization, regional health. Powers the global ops dashboard.","boundaries":"Receives only pre-aggregated, PII-stripped event summaries. Never queries regional databases directly."},{"name":"Regional Event Bus","responsibility":"Intra-region async messaging (order events, courier assignments, notifications). Powers eventual consistency within the region and feeds the regional-to-global event bridge.","boundaries":"Events stay within region by default. Only explicitly whitelisted, anonymized event summaries are forwarded to the global event mesh."},{"name":"Global Event Mesh (Bridge)","responsibility":"Connects regional event buses for cross-region metric aggregation. Implements a strict filter/transform layer that strips PII before forwarding events globally.","boundaries":"PII firewall — transformation is mandatory, not optional. Only anonymized operational events (counts, latencies, statuses) cross region boundaries."},{"name":"Regional Database Cluster","responsibility":"Primary read/write database per region. Multi-AZ replication within the region for durability and failover. Stores customer data, courier profiles, orders, assignments.","boundaries":"Never replicated cross-region. Backups stored within the same legal jurisdiction."},{"name":"Regional Cache Layer","responsibility":"In-region Redis/Memcached for hot data: session tokens, courier availability, active delivery tracking. Supports < 100ms response times.","boundaries":"Region-scoped. Cache invalidation is intra-region only."},{"name":"Degraded Mode Service (per region)","responsibility":"Static fallback + read-from-replica capability. When the primary regional stack is unhealthy, serves cached order status, cached courier locations, and queues new requests for replay. No new assignments but existing deliveries continue tracking.","boundaries":"Activated by regional health checks. Operates on read replicas and local cache. Write operations are queued, not dropped."}],"interfaces":[{"from":"Global DNS + CDN Layer","to":"Regional API Gateway","contract":"GeoDNS latency-based routing. Customer IP → nearest region. Override via explicit region header for migrated accounts.","coupling_type":"loose — DNS only, no runtime dependency"},{"from":"Regional API Gateway","to":"Regional Application Services","contract":"REST/gRPC APIs. All requests carry region context and authenticated identity. Gateway enforces that customer region matches deployment region.","coupling_type":"tight — co-deployed within region"},{"from":"Regional Event Bus","to":"Global Event Mesh (Bridge)","contract":"Async event stream. Events pass through PII-stripping transformer before leaving region. Schema: {event_type, region, timestamp, anonymized_metrics}. No customer_id, no names, no addresses.","coupling_type":"loose — async, fire-and-forget with at-least-once delivery"},{"from":"Global Event Mesh (Bridge)","to":"Global Operations Dashboard (Aggregator)","contract":"Aggregated metric stream consumed via subscription. Dashboard polls/subscribes for real-time updates. Protocol: WebSocket for live dashboard, batch API for historical queries.","coupling_type":"loose — consumer-driven, no back-pressure to regions"},{"from":"Global Control Plane","to":"Regional Data Plane","contract":"Health check heartbeats (region → control plane). Configuration pushes (control plane → region). No PII in either direction. If control plane is unreachable, regions operate autonomously with last-known config.","coupling_type":"loose — regions are autonomous, control plane is advisory"},{"from":"Regional Application Services","to":"Regional Database Cluster","contract":"Repository pattern. Application services access data only through aggregate repositories. No cross-region database connections.","coupling_type":"tight — within region, by design"},{"from":"Regional Application Services","to":"Regional Cache Layer","contract":"Cache-aside pattern for reads. Write-through for courier availability. TTLs enforce freshness.","coupling_type":"moderate — performance dependency, graceful degradation if cache fails"}],"data_flow":"1. Customer request → GeoDNS resolves to nearest regional API gateway. 2. Gateway authenticates, validates geo-residency, routes to regional services. 3. Services execute business logic against regional DB + cache. Response returned < 100ms. 4. State changes emit domain events to regional event bus. 5. Regional event bus triggers local side effects (notifications, tracking updates). 6. PII-stripping bridge transformer selects operational events, anonymizes them, forwards to global event mesh. 7. Global dashboard aggregator consumes anonymized events, updates real-time metrics. 8. Global control plane monitors regional health via heartbeats, distributes config changes.","patterns_used":[{"pattern":"Data Sovereignty / Regional Sharding","rationale":"GDPR and CCPA mandate that customer data stays in its legal jurisdiction. Each region is a sovereign data shard — no cross-region replication of PII."},{"pattern":"Cell-Based Architecture","rationale":"Each region is an independent cell with its own full stack. Cell isolation means a failure in EU doesn't cascade to US or APAC. Aligns with the requirement for regional fault tolerance."},{"pattern":"Event-Driven Architecture with PII Firewall","rationale":"Global dashboard needs real-time visibility without accessing PII. Async event streams with mandatory PII stripping at the region boundary solve both the latency and compliance requirements."},{"pattern":"Bulkhead Pattern","rationale":"Regional isolation acts as a bulkhead. Each region has independent resource pools, preventing noisy-neighbor problems across regions."},{"pattern":"Graceful Degradation / Circuit Breaker","rationale":"When a region's primary services fail, the degraded mode service activates — serving cached reads and queuing writes. Customers see reduced functionality rather than total outage."},{"pattern":"Cache-Aside with Write-Through","rationale":"Sub-100ms response times require aggressive caching of hot paths (courier availability, active delivery tracking) within each region."},{"pattern":"CQRS (for global dashboard)","rationale":"The global dashboard is a pure read model built from event projections. It never writes back to regional systems. Separating the read path for global aggregation from the write path in regional services keeps them decoupled."}]},"domain_model":{"bounded_contexts":[{"name":"Customer Management","classification":"core","aggregates":["Customer (root: customer_id, contains: addresses, payment_methods, communication_preferences)","CustomerRegionPolicy (enforces data residency rules — a Customer is permanently bound to a region)"]},{"name":"Order Lifecycle","classification":"core","aggregates":["Order (root: order_id, contains: line_items, delivery_address, status_history)","DeliveryWindow (value object: time slot, region constraints)"]},{"name":"Courier Operations","classification":"core","aggregates":["Courier (root: courier_id, contains: operating_regions, availability_schedule, current_location)","Assignment (root: assignment_id, links: order_id + courier_id, enforces geo-fence invariant — courier.operating_region must match order.delivery_region)"]},{"name":"Global Operations Intelligence","classification":"supporting","aggregates":["RegionalMetricSnapshot (root: region + timestamp, contains: order_counts, avg_delivery_time, courier_utilization, SLA_compliance)","RegionHealth (root: region_id, contains: health_status, last_heartbeat, degradation_level)"]},{"name":"Notifications","classification":"generic","aggregates":["NotificationTemplate","DeliveryChannel (email, SMS, push — region-specific provider selection)"]},{"name":"Identity & Access","classification":"generic","aggregates":["UserIdentity","RegionBinding (links user to their data-residency region at registration time)"]}],"context_map":[{"upstream":"Customer Management","downstream":"Order Lifecycle","relationship":"Customer-Supplier. Order Lifecycle consumes customer delivery addresses. Customer Management owns the model; Order Lifecycle conforms."},{"upstream":"Order Lifecycle","downstream":"Courier Operations","relationship":"Customer-Supplier. Orders publish delivery requests; Courier Operations consumes them for assignment. Anti-Corruption Layer in Courier Operations translates order concepts to assignment concepts."},{"upstream":"Courier Operations","downstream":"Global Operations Intelligence","relationship":"Published Language via anonymized events. Courier Operations publishes operational metrics; Global Ops consumes them. PII firewall acts as an Anti-Corruption Layer."},{"upstream":"Order Lifecycle","downstream":"Global Operations Intelligence","relationship":"Published Language via anonymized events. Same PII firewall pattern."},{"upstream":"Identity & Access","downstream":"Customer Management","relationship":"Shared Kernel. Region binding is shared between IAM and Customer Management to enforce residency at authentication time."},{"upstream":"Order Lifecycle","downstream":"Notifications","relationship":"Customer-Supplier. Order events trigger notifications. Notifications is a generic context — it conforms to whatever events Order Lifecycle publishes."}]},"trade_offs":[{"decision":"Fully independent regional deployments (cell architecture) rather than a single global deployment with data partitioning","gains":"Hard compliance guarantees — data physically cannot leave the region. Independent failure domains. Independent scaling. Independent deployment cadence per region if needed.","sacrifices":"Operational complexity: 3x the infrastructure to manage. Schema migrations must be coordinated across regions. Feature parity across regions requires discipline.","alternatives_considered":["Single global deployment with row-level data partitioning and query-time filtering — rejected because a misconfigured query could leak cross-region data, and auditors require physical separation","Two regions (EU + RoW) — rejected because CCPA requires US data residency and APAC latency would suffer"]},{"decision":"Async event mesh for global dashboard rather than cross-region database reads","gains":"No PII crosses region boundaries. Dashboard latency is decoupled from regional DB performance. Regions remain autonomous.","sacrifices":"Dashboard shows eventually consistent data (seconds of lag). Complex event schema management across regions.","alternatives_considered":["Cross-region read replicas with PII masking — rejected because masked replicas still technically move encrypted PII across borders, creating audit risk","Periodic batch ETL — rejected because 'real time' requirement demands sub-minute freshness"]},{"decision":"Geo-fence invariant enforced in the Assignment aggregate rather than at the API gateway","gains":"Domain integrity — the business rule lives in the domain model where it belongs. Cannot be bypassed by internal services or batch jobs.","sacrifices":"Slightly more complex domain model. Gateway-level rejection would fail faster for obvious violations.","alternatives_considered":["API gateway geo-validation only — rejected because it's a leaky abstraction; any internal service could bypass it","Both gateway AND domain validation — this is actually the recommended approach: gateway for fast feedback, domain for invariant protection"]},{"decision":"Degraded mode via cached reads + write queuing rather than cross-region failover","gains":"No compliance violation — EU traffic never routes to US infrastructure. Customers get read access to recent data. Writes are preserved for replay.","sacrifices":"Degraded mode cannot process new courier assignments or complete deliveries. Duration of degraded mode is limited by cache freshness and queue capacity.","alternatives_considered":["Cross-region failover (EU fails over to US) — rejected outright because it violates GDPR data residency","Multi-AZ only with no degraded mode — insufficient because AZ failure within a region would cause total outage"]}],"flexibility_points":[{"area":"Adding new regions (e.g., Middle East, South America)","evolution_path":"Cell architecture is designed for replication. A new region is a new cell deployment with its own data plane. Global event mesh and control plane need minimal changes — just subscribe to the new region's event stream."},{"area":"Per-region regulatory customization","evolution_path":"Each region can evolve its data retention policies, consent mechanisms, and notification channels independently. The cell boundary is the customization boundary."},{"area":"Global dashboard analytics evolution","evolution_path":"The CQRS read model for the dashboard can be rebuilt from event history. New metrics, new visualizations, or ML-based predictions can be added by deploying new event consumers without touching regional services."},{"area":"Courier marketplace expansion","evolution_path":"The Assignment aggregate's geo-fence invariant can evolve to support border regions, multi-region couriers, or temporary cross-region authorization — all within the domain model without architectural changes."}],"rigidity_points":[{"area":"Data residency boundaries","reason":"By design, these are rigid. Customer PII is physically bound to a region. Moving a customer between regions is a deliberate, auditable migration — not a configuration change. This rigidity is the compliance guarantee."},{"area":"PII firewall at regional event bridge","reason":"The transformation layer that strips PII before events leave a region must be rigid and auditable. Any change to event schemas must pass through compliance review. This is a security boundary, not a convenience boundary."},{"area":"Customer-to-region binding","reason":"Once a customer is bound to a region at registration, changing it requires a formal data migration process. This is intentionally hard to change to prevent accidental data residency violations."}],"risks":[{"risk":"Schema drift across regions — if regional deployments evolve independently, event schemas and API contracts may diverge, breaking the global dashboard or cross-region coordination.","severity":"high","mitigation":"Shared schema registry with backward-compatibility validation in CI. Schema changes require compatibility checks before deployment to any region. Use semantic versioning for event schemas."},{"risk":"Degraded mode data staleness — if an EU regional outage lasts hours, cached data becomes increasingly stale, and the write queue may overflow.","severity":"high","mitigation":"Define explicit SLOs for degraded mode: max duration (e.g., 4 hours), max queue depth. Beyond these limits, display an explicit 'service unavailable' rather than serving dangerously stale data. Size the queue for 4x normal write volume over the SLO window."},{"risk":"Operational complexity of 3 independent deployments — 3x the monitoring, 3x the on-call, 3x the deployment pipelines.","severity":"medium","mitigation":"Invest in Infrastructure-as-Code with parameterized regional templates. Single deployment pipeline with region as a parameter. Unified observability platform (the global dashboard itself). Follow-the-sun on-call rotation that maps to regions."},{"risk":"Latency budget pressure — 100ms end-to-end is tight when you include DNS resolution, TLS handshake, API gateway, service logic, DB query, and response serialization.","severity":"medium","mitigation":"Keep persistent connections (HTTP/2, connection pooling). Aggressive cache-aside for read-heavy paths. DB query budget: 30ms. Service logic budget: 20ms. Network overhead budget: 50ms. Profile and enforce with p99 latency alarms."},{"risk":"Global event mesh becomes a single point of failure for the dashboard — if the mesh goes down, ops loses global visibility.","severity":"medium","mitigation":"Dashboard should tolerate mesh outages by showing last-known metrics with staleness indicators. Each region also has a local ops dashboard for regional visibility during mesh outages. Mesh itself should be multi-AZ within a neutral region or run as a managed service (e.g., Confluent Cloud, AWS EventBridge Global Endpoints)."},{"risk":"Geo-fence bypass through internal APIs or batch jobs — if the Assignment invariant is only enforced at the API layer, internal systems could create invalid assignments.","severity":"critical","mitigation":"Enforce geo-fence in the Assignment aggregate root's creation logic. The invariant is checked in the domain model, making it impossible to create an invalid assignment through any code path. Additionally, add a database-level check constraint as defense-in-depth."}],"implementation_guidance":{"recommended_approach":"Phase the rollout: (1) Extract the existing EU monolith into the cell architecture pattern with regional event bus. (2) Deploy US cell as a fresh instance with data migration tooling for any US customers currently in the EU deployment. (3) Deploy APAC cell. (4) Build global event mesh and dashboard last — it's read-only and can be added without disrupting regional operations. Each phase is independently valuable and shippable.","files_to_create_or_modify":["infra/modules/regional-cell/ — Terraform/Pulumi module for a complete regional cell (VPC, compute, DB, cache, event bus)","infra/modules/global-mesh/ — Global event mesh infrastructure (cross-region event bridge, PII transformer, dashboard aggregator)","services/assignment/domain/assignment.ts — Assignment aggregate with geo-fence invariant: courier.operatingRegion must match order.deliveryRegion","services/event-bridge/transformers/pii-stripper.ts — Mandatory PII removal before events leave regional boundary. Allowlist-based: only explicitly approved fields pass through.","services/gateway/middleware/geo-validator.ts — API gateway middleware that validates request region matches deployment region","services/degraded-mode/cached-reader.ts — Read-from-cache service activated during regional primary failures","services/degraded-mode/write-queue.ts — Durable write queue for operations during degraded mode, with replay capability","global-dashboard/aggregator/ — Event consumer that builds the global read model from anonymized regional events","docs/adr/001-multi-region-cell-architecture.md","docs/adr/002-data-residency-enforcement.md","docs/adr/003-global-dashboard-cqrs.md","docs/runbook/regional-failover.md","docs/runbook/degraded-mode-operations.md"],"patterns_to_follow":"Cell-based architecture for regional isolation. Domain-driven design with aggregates enforcing geo-fence invariants. CQRS for global read model. Event-driven integration with PII firewall at region boundaries. Cache-aside for latency-sensitive reads. Circuit breaker for degraded mode activation. Infrastructure-as-Code with parameterized regional templates.","pitfalls_to_avoid":["DO NOT use cross-region database replication for customer data — this violates data residency requirements regardless of encryption","DO NOT rely solely on API gateway for geo-fence enforcement — domain invariants must be in the aggregate","DO NOT send raw domain events across regions — all cross-region events must pass through the PII-stripping transformer","DO NOT build the global dashboard by querying regional databases — use event-sourced projections only","DO NOT attempt cross-region failover for customer-facing traffic — degraded mode within-region is the correct pattern","DO NOT share a single database cluster across regions even with read replicas — physical separation is required for compliance","AVOID synchronous cross-region calls — they add latency, create coupling, and complicate failure handling"]},"escalations":[],"confidence":{"level":88,"high_confidence_areas":["Data residency enforcement via cell architecture — this is a well-established pattern for GDPR/CCPA compliance","Geo-fence invariant in the Assignment aggregate — clear domain rule, clear enforcement point","Event-driven global dashboard with PII firewall — standard CQRS pattern applied to a compliance-sensitive context","Degraded mode design — within-region failover with cached reads is the only compliant option"],"low_confidence_areas":["Exact latency budget breakdown (DNS 10ms, TLS 15ms, gateway 5ms, service 20ms, DB 30ms, network 20ms) — needs empirical validation with actual infrastructure in target regions","APAC-specific regulatory requirements — assumed no data residency law as strict as GDPR/CCPA, but this should be validated with legal","Write queue sizing for degraded mode — depends heavily on actual write volume patterns which are unknown"],"assumptions":["APAC does not currently have data residency requirements as strict as GDPR/CCPA (if it does, the same cell pattern applies)","The existing EU deployment can be refactored into the cell architecture pattern without a full rewrite","The global operations dashboard requires near-real-time (seconds) not true real-time (sub-second) metrics","Customer-to-region binding is determined at registration time based on the customer's legal jurisdiction, not their physical location at request time"]}}

Schema migrations must be coordinated across regions. Feature parity across regions requires discipline.","alternatives_considered":["Single global deployment with row-level data partitioning and query-time filtering — rejected because a misconfigured query could leak cross-region data, and auditors require physical separation","Two regions (EU + RoW) — rejected because CCPA requires US data residency and APAC latency would suffer"]},{"decision":"Async event mesh for global dashboard rather than cross-region database reads","gains":"No PII crosses region boundaries. Dashboard latency is decoupled from regional DB performance. Regions remain autonomous.","sacrifices":"Dashboard shows eventually consistent data (seconds of lag). Complex event schema management across regions.","alternatives_considered":["Cross-region read replicas with PII masking — rejected because masked replicas still technically move encrypted PII across borders, creating audit risk","Periodic batch ETL — rejected because 'real time' requirement demands sub-minute freshness"]},{"decision":"Geo-fence invariant enforced in the Assignment aggregate rather than at the API gateway","gains":"Domain integrity — the business rule lives in the domain model where it belongs. Cannot be bypassed by internal services or batch jobs.","sacrifices":"Slightly more complex domain model. Gateway-level rejection would fail faster for obvious violations.","alternatives_considered":["API gateway geo-validation only — rejected because it's a leaky abstraction; any internal service could bypass it","Both gateway AND domain validation — this is actually the recommended approach: gateway for fast feedback, domain for invariant protection"]},{"decision":"Degraded mode via cached reads + write queuing rather than cross-region failover","gains":"No compliance violation — EU traffic never routes to US infrastructure. Customers get read access to recent data. Writes are preserved for replay.","sacrifices":"Degraded mode cannot process new courier assignments or complete deliveries. Duration of degraded mode is limited by cache freshness and queue capacity.","alternatives_considered":["Cross-region failover (EU fails over to US) — rejected outright because it violates GDPR data residency","Multi-AZ only with no degraded mode — insufficient because AZ failure within a region would cause total outage"]}],"flexibility_points":[{"area":"Adding new regions (e.g., Middle East, South America)","evolution_path":"Cell architecture is designed for replication. A new region is a new cell deployment with its own data plane. Global event mesh and control plane need minimal changes — just subscribe to the new region's event stream."},{"area":"Per-region regulatory customization","evolution_path":"Each region can evolve its data retention policies, consent mechanisms, and notification channels independently. The cell boundary is the customization boundary."},{"area":"Global dashboard analytics evolution","evolution_path":"The CQRS read model for the dashboard can be rebuilt from event history. New metrics, new visualizations, or ML-based predictions can be added by deploying new event consumers without touching regional services."},{"area":"Courier marketplace expansion","evolution_path":"The Assignment aggregate's geo-fence invariant can evolve to support border regions, multi-region couriers, or temporary cross-region authorization — all within the domain model without architectural changes."}],"rigidity_points":[{"area":"Data residency boundaries","reason":"By design, these are rigid. Customer PII is physically bound to a region. Moving a customer between regions is a deliberate, auditable migration — not a configuration change. This rigidity is the compliance guarantee."},{"area":"PII firewall at regional event bridge","reason":"The transformation layer that strips PII before events leave a region must be rigid and auditable. Any change to event schemas must pass through compliance review. This is a security boundary, not a convenience boundary."},{"area":"Customer-to-region binding","reason":"Once a customer is bound to a region at registration, changing it requires a formal data migration process. This is intentionally hard to change to prevent accidental data residency violations."}],"risks":[{"risk":"Schema drift across regions — if regional deployments evolve independently, event schemas and API contracts may diverge, breaking the global dashboard or cross-region coordination.","severity":"high","mitigation":"Shared schema registry with backward-compatibility validation in CI. Schema changes require compatibility checks before deployment to any region. Use semantic versioning for event schemas."},{"risk":"Degraded mode data staleness — if an EU regional outage lasts hours, cached data becomes increasingly stale, and the write queue may overflow.","severity":"high","mitigation":"Define explicit SLOs for degraded mode: max duration (e.g., 4 hours), max queue depth. Beyond these limits, display an explicit 'service unavailable' rather than serving dangerously stale data. Size the queue for 4x normal write volume over the SLO window."},{"risk":"Operational complexity of 3 independent deployments — 3x the monitoring, 3x the on-call, 3x the deployment pipelines.","severity":"medium","mitigation":"Invest in Infrastructure-as-Code with parameterized regional templates. Single deployment pipeline with region as a parameter. Unified observability platform (the global dashboard itself). Follow-the-sun on-call rotation that maps to regions."},{"risk":"Latency budget pressure — 100ms end-to-end is tight when you include DNS resolution, TLS handshake, API gateway, service logic, DB query, and response serialization.","severity":"medium","mitigation":"Keep persistent connections (HTTP/2, connection pooling). Aggressive cache-aside for read-heavy paths. DB query budget: 30ms. Service logic budget: 20ms. Network overhead budget: 50ms. Profile and enforce with p99 latency alarms."},{"risk":"Global event mesh becomes a single point of failure for the dashboard — if the mesh goes down, ops loses global visibility.","severity":"medium","mitigation":"Dashboard should tolerate mesh outages by showing last-known metrics with staleness indicators. Each region also has a local ops dashboard for regional visibility during mesh outages. Mesh itself should be multi-AZ within a neutral region or run as a managed service (e.g., Confluent Cloud, AWS EventBridge Global Endpoints)."},{"risk":"Geo-fence bypass through internal APIs or batch jobs — if the Assignment invariant is only enforced at the API layer, internal systems could create invalid assignments.","severity":"critical","mitigation":"Enforce geo-fence in the Assignment aggregate root's creation logic. The invariant is checked in the domain model, making it impossible to create an invalid assignment through any code path. Additionally, add a database-level check constraint as defense-in-depth."}],"implementation_guidance":{"recommended_approach":"Phase the rollout: (1) Extract the existing EU monolith into the cell architecture pattern with regional event bus. (2) Deploy US cell as a fresh instance with data migration tooling for any US customers currently in the EU deployment. (3) Deploy APAC cell. (4) Build global event mesh and dashboard last — it's read-only and can be added without disrupting regional operations. Each phase is independently valuable and shippable.","files_to_create_or_modify":["infra/modules/regional-cell/ — Terraform/Pulumi module for a complete regional cell (VPC, compute, DB, cache, event bus)","infra/modules/global-mesh/ — Global event mesh infrastructure (cross-region event bridge, PII transformer, dashboard aggregator)","services/assignment/domain/assignment.ts — Assignment aggregate with geo-fence invariant: courier.operatingRegion must match order.deliveryRegion","services/event-bridge/transformers/pii-stripper.ts — Mandatory PII removal before events leave regional boundary. Allowlist-based: only explicitly approved fields pass through.","services/gateway/middleware/geo-validator.ts — API gateway middleware that validates request region matches deployment region","services/degraded-mode/cached-reader.ts — Read-from-cache service activated during regional primary failures","services/degraded-mode/write-queue.ts — Durable write queue for operations during degraded mode, with replay capability","global-dashboard/aggregator/ — Event consumer that builds the global read model from anonymized regional events","docs/adr/001-multi-region-cell-architecture.md","docs/adr/002-data-residency-enforcement.md","docs/adr/003-global-dashboard-cqrs.md","docs/runbook/regional-failover.md","docs/runbook/degraded-mode-operations.md"],"patterns_to_follow":"Cell-based architecture for regional isolation. Domain-driven design with aggregates enforcing geo-fence invariants. CQRS for global read model. Event-driven integration with PII firewall at region boundaries. Cache-aside for latency-sensitive reads. Circuit breaker for degraded mode activation. Infrastructure-as-Code with parameterized regional templates.","pitfalls_to_avoid":["DO NOT use cross-region database replication for customer data — this violates data residency requirements regardless of encryption","DO NOT rely solely on API gateway for geo-fence enforcement — domain invariants must be in the aggregate","DO NOT send raw domain events across regions — all cross-region events must pass through the PII-stripping transformer","DO NOT build the global dashboard by querying regional databases — use event-sourced projections only","DO NOT attempt cross-region failover for customer-facing traffic — degraded mode within-region is the correct pattern","DO NOT share a single database cluster across regions even with read replicas — physical separation is required for compliance","AVOID synchronous cross-region calls — they add latency, create coupling, and complicate failure handling"]},"escalations":[],"confidence":{"level":88,"high_confidence_areas":["Data residency enforcement via cell architecture — this is a well-established pattern for GDPR/CCPA compliance","Geo-fence invariant in the Assignment aggregate — clear domain rule, clear enforcement point","Event-driven global dashboard with PII firewall — standard CQRS pattern applied to a compliance-sensitive context","Degraded mode design — within-region failover with cached reads is the only compliant option"],"low_confidence_areas":["Exact latency budget breakdown (DNS 10ms, TLS 15ms, gateway 5ms, service 20ms, DB 30ms, network 20ms) — needs empirical validation with actual infrastructure in target regions","APAC-specific regulatory requirements — assumed no data residency law as strict as GDPR/CCPA, but this should be validated with legal","Write queue sizing for degraded mode — depends heavily on actual write volume patterns which are unknown"],"assumptions":["APAC does not currently have data residency requirements as strict as GDPR/CCPA (if it does, the same cell pattern applies)","The existing EU deployment can be refactored into the cell architecture pattern without a full rewrite","The global operations dashboard requires near-real-time (seconds) not true real-time (sub-second) metrics","Customer-to-region binding is determined at registration time based on the customer's legal jurisdiction, not their physical location at request time"]}}

trace.result: {"executive_summary":"Multi-region logistics platform architecture expanding from single-region EU to EU + US + APAC. The design uses region-sovereign data planes for GDPR/CCPA compliance, a lightweight global control plane for cross-region coordination, and an asynchronous event mesh for the global operations dashboard. Each region is a self-contained deployment that owns its customer and courier data, enforces geo-fencing at the domain level, and can degrade gracefully during outages. The global layer is read-only and aggregation-focused — it never stores PII.","architecture":{"components":[{"name":"Regional Data Plane (x3: EU, US, APAC)","responsibility":"Full-stack deployment per region: API gateway, application services, database, cache, message broker. Owns all customer PII, courier assignments, and order lifecycle for that region. Serves < 100ms latency via regional proximity.","boundaries":"Hard boundary at region. No cross-region synchronous calls. Customer and courier data never replicated outside the region. Each plane is independently deployable and can operate in isolation."},{"name":"Regional API Gateway","responsibility":"Routes customer/courier requests to regional services. Enforces authentication, rate limiting, and geo-routing. Performs initial geo-validation — rejects requests that arrive at the wrong region.","boundaries":"Region-scoped. Sits behind a global DNS/CDN layer that routes by geography."},{"name":"Global DNS + CDN Layer","responsibility":"GeoDNS (e.g., Route 53 latency-based routing) directs customers to their nearest regional API gateway. CDN caches static assets globally.","boundaries":"No PII passes through CDN. DNS is the only truly global routing component."},{"name":"Global Control Plane","responsibility":"Lightweight service for cross-region coordination: region health monitoring, global configuration distribution, region-to-region escalation (e.g., support tickets that cross regions). Stores NO customer PII — only anonymized operational metadata.","boundaries":"Read-only access to regional event streams. Cannot write to regional data stores. Deployed multi-region for its own availability."},{"name":"Global Operations Dashboard (Aggregator)","responsibility":"Consumes anonymized/aggregated metric streams from all regions. Provides real-time KPIs: deliveries in progress, SLA compliance, courier utilization, regional health. Powers the global ops dashboard.","boundaries":"Receives only pre-aggregated, PII-stripped event summaries. Never queries regional databases directly."},{"name":"Regional Event Bus","responsibility":"Intra-region async messaging (order events, courier assignments, notifications). Powers eventual consistency within the region and feeds the regional-to-global event bridge.","boundaries":"Events stay within region by default. Only explicitly whitelisted, anonymized event summaries are forwarded to the global event mesh."},{"name":"Global Event Mesh (Bridge)","responsibility":"Connects regional event buses for cross-region metric aggregation. Implements a strict filter/transform layer that strips PII before forwarding events globally.","boundaries":"PII firewall — transformation is mandatory, not optional. Only anonymized operational events (counts, latencies, statuses) cross region boundaries."},{"name":"Regional Database Cluster","responsibility":"Primary read/write database per region. Multi-AZ replication within the region for durability and failover. Stores customer data, courier profiles, orders, assignments.","boundaries":"Never replicated cross-region. Backups stored within the same legal jurisdiction."},{"name":"Regional Cache Layer","responsibility":"In-region Redis/Memcached for hot data: session tokens, courier availability, active delivery tracking. Supports < 100ms response times.","boundaries":"Region-scoped. Cache invalidation is intra-region only."},{"name":"Degraded Mode Service (per region)","responsibility":"Static fallback + read-from-replica capability. When the primary regional stack is unhealthy, serves cached order status, cached courier locations, and queues new requests for replay. No new assignments but existing deliveries continue tracking.","boundaries":"Activated by regional health checks. Operates on read replicas and local cache. Write operations are queued, not dropped."}],"interfaces":[{"from":"Global DNS + CDN Layer","to":"Regional API Gateway","contract":"GeoDNS latency-based routing. Customer IP → nearest region. Override via explicit region header for migrated accounts.","coupling_type":"loose — DNS only, no runtime dependency"},{"from":"Regional API Gateway","to":"Regional Application Services","contract":"REST/gRPC APIs. All requests carry region context and authenticated identity. Gateway enforces that customer region matches deployment region.","coupling_type":"tight — co-deployed within region"},{"from":"Regional Event Bus","to":"Global Event Mesh (Bridge)","contract":"Async event stream. Events pass through PII-stripping transformer before leaving region. Schema: {event_type, region, timestamp, anonymized_metrics}. No customer_id, no names, no addresses.","coupling_type":"loose — async, fire-and-forget with at-least-once delivery"},{"from":"Global Event Mesh (Bridge)","to":"Global Operations Dashboard (Aggregator)","contract":"Aggregated metric stream consumed via subscription. Dashboard polls/subscribes for real-time updates. Protocol: WebSocket for live dashboard, batch API for historical queries.","coupling_type":"loose — consumer-driven, no back-pressure to regions"},{"from":"Global Control Plane","to":"Regional Data Plane","contract":"Health check heartbeats (region → control plane). Configuration pushes (control plane → region). No PII in either direction. If control plane is unreachable, regions operate autonomously with last-known config.","coupling_type":"loose — regions are autonomous, control plane is advisory"},{"from":"Regional Application Services","to":"Regional Database Cluster","contract":"Repository pattern. Application services access data only through aggregate repositories. No cross-region database connections.","coupling_type":"tight — within region, by design"},{"from":"Regional Application Services","to":"Regional Cache Layer","contract":"Cache-aside pattern for reads. Write-through for courier availability. TTLs enforce freshness.","coupling_type":"moderate — performance dependency, graceful degradation if cache fails"}],"data_flow":"1. Customer request → GeoDNS resolves to nearest regional API gateway. 2. Gateway authenticates, validates geo-residency, routes to regional services. 3. Services execute business logic against regional DB + cache. Response returned < 100ms. 4. State changes emit domain events to regional event bus. 5. Regional event bus triggers local side effects (notifications, tracking updates). 6. PII-stripping bridge transformer selects operational events, anonymizes them, forwards to global event mesh. 7. Global dashboard aggregator consumes anonymized events, updates real-time metrics. 8. Global control plane monitors regional health via heartbeats, distributes config changes.","patterns_used":[{"pattern":"Data Sovereignty / Regional Sharding","rationale":"GDPR and CCPA mandate that customer data stays in its legal jurisdiction. Each region is a sovereign data shard — no cross-region replication of PII."},{"pattern":"Cell-Based Architecture","rationale":"Each region is an independent cell with its own full stack. Cell isolation means a failure in EU doesn't cascade to US or APAC. Aligns with the requirement for regional fault tolerance."},{"pattern":"Event-Driven Architecture with PII Firewall","rationale":"Global dashboard needs real-time visibility without accessing PII. Async event streams with mandatory PII stripping at the region boundary solve both the latency and compliance requirements."},{"pattern":"Bulkhead Pattern","rationale":"Regional isolation acts as a bulkhead. Each region has independent resource pools, preventing noisy-neighbor problems across regions."},{"pattern":"Graceful Degradation / Circuit Breaker","rationale":"When a region's primary services fail, the degraded mode service activates — serving cached reads and queuing writes. Customers see reduced functionality rather than total outage."},{"pattern":"Cache-Aside with Write-Through","rationale":"Sub-100ms response times require aggressive caching of hot paths (courier availability, active delivery tracking) within each region."},{"pattern":"CQRS (for global dashboard)","rationale":"The global dashboard is a pure read model built from event projections. It never writes back to regional systems. Separating the read path for global aggregation from the write path in regional services keeps them decoupled."}]},"domain_model":{"bounded_contexts":[{"name":"Customer Management","classification":"core","aggregates":["Customer (root: customer_id, contains: addresses, payment_methods, communication_preferences)","CustomerRegionPolicy (enforces data residency rules — a Customer is permanently bound to a region)"]},{"name":"Order Lifecycle","classification":"core","aggregates":["Order (root: order_id, contains: line_items, delivery_address, status_history)","DeliveryWindow (value object: time slot, region constraints)"]},{"name":"Courier Operations","classification":"core","aggregates":["Courier (root: courier_id, contains: operating_regions, availability_schedule, current_location)","Assignment (root: assignment_id, links: order_id + courier_id, enforces geo-fence invariant — courier.operating_region must match order.delivery_region)"]},{"name":"Global Operations Intelligence","classification":"supporting","aggregates":["RegionalMetricSnapshot (root: region + timestamp, contains: order_counts, avg_delivery_time, courier_utilization, SLA_compliance)","RegionHealth (root: region_id, contains: health_status, last_heartbeat, degradation_level)"]},{"name":"Notifications","classification":"generic","aggregates":["NotificationTemplate","DeliveryChannel (email, SMS, push — region-specific provider selection)"]},{"name":"Identity & Access","classification":"generic","aggregates":["UserIdentity","RegionBinding (links user to their data-residency region at registration time)"]}],"context_map":[{"upstream":"Customer Management","downstream":"Order Lifecycle","relationship":"Customer-Supplier. Order Lifecycle consumes customer delivery addresses. Customer Management owns the model; Order Lifecycle conforms."},{"upstream":"Order Lifecycle","downstream":"Courier Operations","relationship":"Customer-Supplier. Orders publish delivery requests; Courier Operations consumes them for assignment. Anti-Corruption Layer in Courier Operations translates order concepts to assignment concepts."},{"upstream":"Courier Operations","downstream":"Global Operations Intelligence","relationship":"Published Language via anonymized events. Courier Operations publishes operational metrics; Global Ops consumes them. PII firewall acts as an Anti-Corruption Layer."},{"upstream":"Order Lifecycle","downstream":"Global Operations Intelligence","relationship":"Published Language via anonymized events. Same PII firewall pattern."},{"upstream":"Identity & Access","downstream":"Customer Management","relationship":"Shared Kernel. Region binding is shared between IAM and Customer Management to enforce residency at authentication time."},{"upstream":"Order Lifecycle","downstream":"Notifications","relationship":"Customer-Supplier. Order events trigger notifications. Notifications is a generic context — it conforms to whatever events Order Lifecycle publishes."}]},"trade_offs":[{"decision":"Fully independent regional deployments (cell architecture) rather than a single global deployment with data partitioning","gains":"Hard compliance guarantees — data physically cannot leave the region. Independent failure domains. Independent scaling. Independent deployment cadence per region if needed.","sacrifices":"Operational complexity: 3x the infrastructure to manage. Schema migrations must be coordinated across regions. Feature parity across regions requires discipline.","alternatives_considered":["Single global deployment with row-level data partitioning and query-time filtering — rejected because a misconfigured query could leak cross-region data, and auditors require physical separation","Two regions (EU + RoW) — rejected because CCPA requires US data residency and APAC latency would suffer"]},{"decision":"Async event mesh for global dashboard rather than cross-region database reads","gains":"No PII crosses region boundaries. Dashboard latency is decoupled from regional DB performance. Regions remain autonomous.","sacrifices":"Dashboard shows eventually consistent data (seconds of lag). Complex event schema management across regions.","alternatives_considered":["Cross-region read replicas with PII masking — rejected because masked replicas still technically move encrypted PII across borders, creating audit risk","Periodic batch ETL — rejected because 'real time' requirement demands sub-minute freshness"]},{"decision":"Geo-fence invariant enforced in the Assignment aggregate rather than at the API gateway","gains":"Domain integrity — the business rule lives in the domain model where it belongs. Cannot be bypassed by internal services or batch jobs.","sacrifices":"Slightly more complex domain model. Gateway-level rejection would fail faster for obvious violations.","alternatives_considered":["API gateway geo-validation only — rejected because it's a leaky abstraction; any internal service could bypass it","Both gateway AND domain validation — this is actually the recommended approach: gateway for fast feedback, domain for invariant protection"]},{"decision":"Degraded mode via cached reads + write queuing rather than cross-region failover","gains":"No compliance violation — EU traffic never routes to US infrastructure. Customers get read access to recent data. Writes are preserved for replay.","sacrifices":"Degraded mode cannot process new courier assignments or complete deliveries. Duration of degraded mode is limited by cache freshness and queue capacity.","alternatives_considered":["Cross-region failover (EU fails over to US) — rejected outright because it violates GDPR data residency","Multi-AZ only with no degraded mode — insufficient because AZ failure within a region would cause total outage"]}],"flexibility_points":[{"area":"Adding new regions (e.g., Middle East, South America)","evolution_path":"Cell architecture is designed for replication. A new region is a new cell deployment with its own data plane. Global event mesh and control plane need minimal changes — just subscribe to the new region's event stream."},{"area":"Per-region regulatory customization","evolution_path":"Each region can evolve its data retention policies, consent mechanisms, and notification channels independently. The cell boundary is the customization boundary."},{"area":"Global dashboard analytics evolution","evolution_path":"The CQRS read model for the dashboard can be rebuilt from event history. New metrics, new visualizations, or ML-based predictions can be added by deploying new event consumers without touching regional services."},{"area":"Courier marketplace expansion","evolution_path":"The Assignment aggregate's geo-fence invariant can evolve to support border regions, multi-region couriers, or temporary cross-region authorization — all within the domain model without architectural changes."}],"rigidity_points":[{"area":"Data residency boundaries","reason":"By design, these are rigid. This rigidity is the compliance guarantee."},{"area":"PII firewall at regional event bridge","reason":"The transformation layer that strips PII before events leave a region must be rigid and auditable.
